— Headline: Effect Sizes and Subgroup Interactions
— Headline: Practice Impact and the Honest Caveat

— Round 4: Liu, Radiomics for CMS4 Colorectal Subtyping

key_claims.claim: CT/CTA/perfusion imaging characteristics may identify MeVO stroke subgroups with differential thrombectomy benefit, based on post-hoc analysis of RCT data.
Commercial AI devices for lung cancer detection on chest radiographs show variable diagnostic accuracy when tested independently at representative prevalence.
AI-based slab reconstruction for digital breast tomosynthesis maintains non-inferior screening performance while reducing image volume.
A Mamba-architecture deep learning model predicts pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy in breast cancer with AUCs of approximately 0.78–0.81 across five external cohorts.
MRI radiomics can predict CMS4 colorectal cancer subtype preoperatively, outperforming deep learning comparators in multicenter testing.
